data.narrative:
Deep Sentinel, a Series B-stage defense and security company founded by David Selinger, has raised $55M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 peers—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private companies in the same sector and stage, including Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Code Metal, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy base and applying a typical 1.9x step-up multiplier for Series B progression, implies a valuation of $104M. Peer-median analysis across 7 same-stage, same-sector comparables yields a median valuation of $1.0B, with the peer set ranging from $300M to $3.0B. These methods produce substantially divergent signals, reflecting meaningful heterogeneity within the Defense & Security frontier-tech segment.

The triangulated valuation range spans $89M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $552M. The wide dispersion between stage-progression and peer-median methods reflects uncertainty about Deep Sentinel's growth trajectory relative to its peer cohort. Key variables driving the range include revenue scale, customer concentration, and technology differentiation versus the peer set. Resolution of Deep Sentinel's competitive positioning within the Defense & Security subsector would materially narrow this band.
